pages.raw_text:
Eugene v. Debs was greeted at the
overflowing house, who listened with
marked appreciation to the speaker
throughout the evening, breaking
forth in thunderous applause several
times when an especially strong
point was scored by the speaker.


: He was briefly introduced to the
impatient audience by d. l.. Bur-
CESS of Tacoma. without useless
preliminary. he entered into his sub
ject, demonstrating that he was thor
oughly familiar with the questions
Of the day, student of social and
political economy and master of
oratory.


Going to the foundation of his SUBJ
ject. he showed how, through all the
centuries of the past, society has
been divided into two classes, the
few who have ruled and the many
who have served. How the present
state of education and enlightenment
was the result of an unremitting
struggle for centuries upon the part
OF the oppressed to throw of the
yoke of bondage. That this is still
progressing and will continue to go
on until every shackle that binds the
real producer in slavery is broken.
and they receive the full measure of
justice by receiving their full share
Of the fruits of their production.


Are the working men. as a class.
ignmant or educated?" was his para
mount question. You pay the sal
aries of the judges-they repay you
by injunction. You make the guns
but II 80 happens that at the critical
moment OF their use you are at the
wrong end? You produce by your
own toll the silks and satins to
clothe the rich-your masters-but
you wear the calico, Is this Justice
to that degree in which you claim?"


To each capitalist In the United
States there are IL wage slaves who
serve him, and add to his power as
master of their destiny by their toll.
Under the plans of socialism these IL
Industrial slaves would become mas
ters instead of menials, The two
great political parties. he said. are
rooted In the same soil-the soil OF
capitalism. No matter which party
you cast your vote for. IL is for one
and the same master-the ruling
class, who absorb but do not pro-
ducc. Both parties conceal the vital
issues, and upon the surface give
forth false issue. Socialism. upon
the other hand, places before the
thinker and the real producer vital
